title: Password Cracking Time Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
description: Free password cracking time estimator. See how long to brute-force any password based on length and character set. Updated for 2026.
intro: Technical calculations in IT and development are critical: latency, bandwidth, uptime SLA, cloud costs, algorithmic complexity. A solid initial estimate prevents over-provisioning infrastructure. This calculator estimates brute-force cracking time based on password length and character set—free and fast. Values are updated for 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you current.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **Attempts = charset^length**. Direct application with your inputs. Estimates brute-force cracking time based on password length and character set.
useCases: Technical calculations related to password cracking time
Teaching and learning password security concepts
explanation:
**Technical calculators** for IT: latency, storage, uptime SLA, cloud costs. Decisions that scale.

## How it's calculated

Estimates brute-force cracking time based on password length and character set—free and accurate.

This calculator applies the formula with your inputs, checking typical ranges and alerting when values fall outside reasonable bounds.

## Final notes

This calculation is a reference estimate. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a qualified professional. Values are updated for 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: Is 1 billion hashes per second realistic?
faq.a: Yes, modern GPUs can compute 1B+ SHA256 hashes per second.

faq.q: Should I use a passphrase instead of a password?
faq.a: Yes, a 4-5 word random passphrase is more secure (see XKCD 936).
2FA adds a critical security layer beyond just password strength.

faq.q: Why should I use a password manager?
faq.a: Password managers generate and securely store unique passwords for each site.

faq.q: What are rainbow tables and how do I protect against them?
faq.a: Rainbow tables pre-compute password hashes. Using a salt prevents them from working.
